text:
Competitors have sweltered in the Olympic Village as the Paris 2024 organisers' move to reduce carbon emissions by initially not including air conditioners has backfired.

The Paris Olympic Games’ goal to limit carbon emissions with self-cooling rooms that do not need air conditioning backfired this week after complaints mounted from competitors when the French capital was hit with a heatwave.

The organisers' original plans for a geothermal system that pipes cold water underneath the floors to keep the rooms six degrees cooler than the outside temperature fell short of countering temperatures upwards of 35C this week.

While richer nations like Australia, the United States, Canada and Japan planned ahead by providing their competitors with portable air conditioning, other nations were left sweating in their accommodation.

The Olympic committee did cave in and ordered 2,500 air conditioning units at the start of the month, however many are still left boiling in the 7000-room Olympic Village.

Sky News Australia's Matt Cunningham, who has been reporting on the scene in Paris, told Andrew Bolt he did not envy many competitors struggling with the tough conditions.

“You wouldn't want to be some of those athletes at the Olympics Village,” he told The Bolt Report on Tuesday evening.

“One of the bizarre things about this is games organisers have been all about being environmentally friendly and being on … a social justice crusade.

"The great twist here is that the people who are going to be in the rooms with no air conditioning are going to be those from the poorer countries.

“The Australians and the United States, they've been able to bring in their own air conditioning, but a lot of those athletes, they come from poor nations (and) their countries haven't been able to afford to do the same thing."

Paris 2024 spokeswoman Anne Deschamps acknowledged competitor complaints and said she wanted to accommodate the needs of Olympians during the heat.

“We have found a balance for the athlete’s village long term responsibility for a model neighbourhood with all of the (green) innovations and our short-term responsibility to give to high performance athletes the best conditions,” she said as reported in the Herald Sun.

Australia prepared for the sweltering heat at the Olympics last year with the Australian Olympic Committee chief executive Matt Carroll telling The Daily Telegraph in November he appreciated the green initiative but emphasised the need to cater for the athletes during the competition.

“This is a high-performance Games. We’re not going for a picnic,” he said.

Competitors from nations that did not bring their own A/C had to reserve an air conditioner ahead of time at their own expense, however the vast disparity between units and rooms has left many in the lurch.

Swiss rower Celia Dupre took to TikTok with a litany of complaints about the Paris 2024 accommodation, taking particular issue with the boiling rooms.

Replying to @user4838113313573 i am aware that it is for the environment but then maybe invest in best where people dont have to buy a matress topper to then leave behind after…#fyp#olympics#paris2024#olympicgames

“There’s no air conditioning, oh come on!” she said in a video posted to TikTok.

“The walls, apparently, have this water system in it that’s supposed to cool the room, but I was on the second floor and it was already hot so I can’t imagine (what) people who are on like the sixth floor (would be feeling).”

Green initiatives at the Olympic Games have been poorly received as the move to serve less meat disgruntled many of the athletes.

Former Australian swimmer James Magnussen sparked the debate on the Matty & The Missile podcast when he hit out at the state of village life.

“I joked last night about the amount of vegan options in the village that they’re running out of meat, well, an Australian heavy weight boxer has come out, he wanted lamb chops, maximum two chops person," he said.

"The guy’s six foot six, he’d be 100 (kg) at the best. Two chops."

The Australian Olympic Committee acknowledged their were some early teething issues, but said food quantities have since been increased.

title: Olympians swelter without air conditioning as 2024 Paris Olympic Games green initiatives fall short for disgruntled athletes | Sky News Australia
description: The Paris Olympic Games’ goal to limit carbon emissions with self-cooling rooms that do not need air conditioning backfired this week after complaints mounted from competitors when the French capital was hit with a heatwave.
keywords: Air conditioning,Matt Cunningham,2024 Paris Olympic Games,Oympics,air conditioning olympic games
